perturbation.original_text: The Parties may withdraw from this JV only for serious and justified reasons or by mutual consent. The withdrawal must be exercised by written notice, to be sent to the other Party by certified letter or PEC, with minimum notice of 30 days.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties may withdraw from this JV at any time and for any reason. The withdrawal must be exercised by written notice, to be sent to the other Party by certified letter or PEC, with minimum notice of 30 days.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'only for serious and justified reasons' to 'at any time and for any reason', we introduce a contradiction. Section 9 now states that a party can withdraw for any reason, which contradicts the implied restriction in the original text. This creates uncertainty regarding the conditions under which a party can withdraw from the agreement. This in-text contradiction has implications in Section 2 of the agreement, making it unclear when certain obligations are regulated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding as referred to the point 1, the Parties wish to collaborate to the JV in the respective R&D areas; for the firsts two clinical trial programs, they undertake to collaborate as follow: Topic 1. Clinical trial program in Oncology. With regard to the JV between the Parties about the Clinical trial program in Oncology, the respective obligations are regulated below: - Molipharma, through a separate agreement with UCSC, undertakes to provide the tissue samples and parts of tumors; - Molipharma undertakes to make UCSC the site for clinical trials and in particular Molipharma undertakes to make UCSC the main site for clinical trials in cancer using -2- the specific isoforms CAR (Chimeric Antigen Receptor) and/or check inhibitor technology, Exhibit A - Kiromic is committed to bear all costs necessary for R&D, including all clinical development costs, according to the terms and conditions set out in point n. 3; Topic 2. Clinical trial program in Covid19 Sars CoV2 Vaccine With regard to the JV between the Parties about the clinical trial program in Covid19 Sars CoV2 Vaccine, the respective obligations are regulated below: - Kiromic is committed to sharing patents and know-how in relation to the following products which will be licensed to the JV exclusively for the application in the specific and limited field of sars-cov-2 threat and relative disease COVID-19: (i) VAPAs-Viral Antigen Proteins Associated © (Kiromic-2020) derived from Diamonds AI - Artificial Intelligence Platform for Discovery and Prediction Antigen Protein (ii) Platform of DC Vaccines (dendritic cell vaccine) - for therapeutic purposes - nominated BSK 01; (iii) Oral Delivery Platform for Prophylactic Vaccine - accompanying immuno-boosting therapy - therapeutic vaccine administration - nominated BSK02 (iv) Other patents eventually applicable in the specific field. - Molipharma provides skills, competencies, relationships, financial resources and means for development; - Molipharma is committed to ensuring that the development and testing of the vaccine and any associated clinical trial studies are carried out through the specialized structures of the UCSC. - Molipharma provides skills, competencies, relationships, financial resources and means for development; - Molipharma is committed to ensuring that the development and testing of the vaccine and any associated clinical trial studies are carried out through the specialized structures of the UCSC. - Molipharma undertakes to make UCSC the site for clinical trials and in particular - Molipharma, through a separate agreement with UCSC, undertakes to provide the biological samples necessary to carry out the Research and Development, such as, but not limited, blood, serum, saliva, clinical data, tissues samples of living and dead patients etc ; - Molipharma is committed to bear all costs necessary for R&D, including all clinical development costs, according to the terms and conditions set out in point n. 3;
perturbation.justification: The original text limited withdrawal to 'serious and justified reasons', creating a defined scope. The changed text allows withdrawal 'at any time and for any reason', creating a direct conflict. This contradicts Section 2, which outlines specific obligations dependent on the JV's continuation. The unlimited withdrawal undermines the enforceability of these obligations, creating uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties undertake, within 30 days from the signing of this JV, to grant a specific written and notarial mandate, which gives Molipharma the power to represent the JV vis-à-vis third parties for the performance of ordinary and extraordinary acts deemed necessary for the quickest and most profitable achievement of the objectives set forth in point 2, including the right to enter into partnership and/or collaboration contracts with external entities.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties undertake, within 30 days from the signing of this JV, to grant a specific written and notarial mandate, which gives Molipharma the power to represent the JV vis-à-vis third parties for the performance of ordinary acts only, excluding extraordinary acts. This representation is strictly limited to administrative tasks and shall not include the right to enter into partnership and/or collaboration contracts with external entities.
perturbation.explanation: By restricting Molipharma's power to only 'ordinary acts' and explicitly excluding 'extraordinary acts' and the ability to enter into partnerships, we create a contradiction with the later clause in Section 3 which gives Molipharma the power to represent the JV vis-à-vis third parties for the performance of ordinary and extraordinary acts deemed necessary for the quickest and most profitable achievement of the objectives set forth in point 2. Now, section 2 introduces ambiguity as to what falls under those terms, ordinary and extraordinay acts
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding as referred to the point 1, the Parties wish to collaborate to the JV in the respective R&D areas; for the firsts two clinical trial programs, they undertake to collaborate as follow: Topic 1. Clinical trial program in Oncology. With regard to the JV between the Parties about the Clinical trial program in Oncology, the respective obligations are regulated below: - Molipharma, through a separate agreement with UCSC, undertakes to provide the tissue samples and parts of tumors; - Molipharma undertakes to make UCSC the site for clinical trials and in particular Molipharma undertakes to make UCSC the main site for clinical trials in cancer using -2- the specific isoforms CAR (Chimeric Antigen Receptor) and/or check inhibitor technology, Exhibit A - Kiromic is committed to bear all costs necessary for R&D, including all clinical development costs, according to the terms and conditions set out in point n. 3; Topic 2. Clinical trial program in Covid19 Sars CoV2 Vaccine With regard to the JV between the Parties about the clinical trial program in Covid19 Sars CoV2 Vaccine, the respective obligations are regulated below: - Kiromic is committed to sharing patents and know-how in relation to the following products which will be licensed to the JV exclusively for the application in the specific and limited field of sars-cov-2 threat and relative disease COVID-19: (i) VAPAs-Viral Antigen Proteins Associated © (Kiromic-2020) derived from Diamonds AI - Artificial Intelligence Platform for Discovery and Prediction Antigen Protein (ii) Platform of DC Vaccines (dendritic cell vaccine) - for therapeutic purposes - nominated BSK 01; (iii) Oral Delivery Platform for Prophylactic Vaccine - accompanying immuno-boosting therapy - therapeutic vaccine administration - nominated BSK02 (iv) Other patents eventually applicable in the specific field. - Molipharma provides skills, competencies, relationships, financial resources and means for development; - Molipharma is committed to ensuring that the development and testing of the vaccine and any associated clinical trial studies are carried out through the specialized structures of the UCSC. - Molipharma provides skills, competencies, relationships, financial resources and means for development; - Molipharma is committed to ensuring that the development and testing of the vaccine and any associated clinical trial studies are carried out through the specialized structures of the UCSC. - Molipharma undertakes to make UCSC the site for clinical trials and in particular - Molipharma, through a separate agreement with UCSC, undertakes to provide the biological samples necessary to carry out the Research and Development, such as, but not limited, blood, serum, saliva, clinical data, tissues samples of living and dead patients etc ; - Molipharma is committed to bear all costs necessary for R&D, including all clinical development costs, according to the terms and conditions set out in point n. 3;
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly limits Molipharma's power to 'ordinary acts only' and excludes 'extraordinary acts' and entering into partnerships. This directly contradicts the contradicted text where Molipharma undertakes to make UCSC the site for clinical trials. This creates a contradiction because Molipharma needs to be able to perform both ordinary and extraordinary acts in order to fulfill the obligations of undertaking the clinical trials at UCSC, creating a significant legal issue. This fits the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: All economics rights are solely owned by Kiromic Biopharma. Kiromic will grant to Molipharma the follows royalties: - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Italy; -7- - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Europe.
perturbation.changed_text: All economics rights are jointly owned by Kiromic Biopharma and Molipharma. The share of economic rights will be decided on a case by case basis. All economics rights are solely owned by Kiromic Biopharma. Kiromic will grant to Molipharma the follows royalties: - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Italy; -7- - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Europe.
perturbation.explanation: Initially the economic rights are solely owned by Kiromic Biopharma. After the perturbation there are two conflicting statements regarding ownership: first, that economic rights are jointly owned and decided on a case by case basis, and then immediately afterwards, it says that ALL economics rights are solely owned by Kiromic Biopharma. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty in section 7 regarding who profits from the research. The in-text contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the financial benefits of the collaboration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ECONOMIC RIGHTS The commercial rights arising from the research programs referred to in point 2 are divided as follows: Oncology All economics rights are jointly owned by Kiromic Biopharma and Molipharma. The share of economic rights will be decided on a case by case basis. All economics rights are solely owned by Kiromic Biopharma. Kiromic will grant to Molipharma the follows royalties: - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Italy; -7- - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Europe. Sars-cov-2 - The economic rights for Europa will be an exclusive ownership of Molipharma - The economic rights in the U.S. will be an exclusive ownership of Kiromic. - For the rest of the world, the economic rights will be divided as follows: *% Kiromic; *% Molipharma.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear in-text contradiction regarding the ownership of economic rights. Initially, the text states that these rights are jointly owned by Kiromic and Molipharma and will be decided on a case-by-case basis. Immediately following, the text states that ALL economic rights are solely owned by Kiromic Biopharma. This directly contradicts the previous statement about joint ownership, creating ambiguity about who has the right to profit from the research, specifically impacting the royalty agreements outlined later in the clause. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition, where inconsistent definitions of key obligations (economic rights) create uncertainty and potential unenforceability.
